name: I got hit by a falling tool on a construction site in Salt Lake City yesterday, what do I do right now?
description: Report to employer now, get medical help, take photos—don't talk to insurance alone.

mainEntity.name: I got hit by a falling tool on a construction site in Salt Lake City yesterday, what do I do right now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to employer now, get medical help, take photos—don't talk to insurance alone.
acceptedAnswer.description: **Report the injury to your employer immediately and seek medical attention to document your injuries.** Construction accidents like falling objects are common hazards under OSHA rules, and in Utah, prompt reporting preserves your workers' comp claim while opening doors to third-party lawsuits if another party was negligent[1][3]. **Key factors:** 1. Notify employer within 180 days for workers' comp eligibility in Utah. 2. Get photos of the site and hazards before changes occur. 3. OSHA/UOSH may investigate if serious. 4. Preserve evidence as sites change fast[3]. Avoid giving statements to insurance without advice; consult a lawyer quickly to evaluate third-party claims against subcontractors or owners[1]. Early action maximizes recovery beyond basic workers' comp.
